rule_text: Statutory limitation period for arrear claims against AP Government for payments made in contingent bills for supplies/services rendered (i.e., contractor bills) is THREE YEARS from the date the amount first became due; for claims under written registered contracts, the period is SIX YEARS.
verification_method: Reject contractor bill arrear claims older than 3 years (or 6 years if written-registered contract) without Government special sanction

rule_text: AP Government servants drawing bills for pay/allowances/contingent expenses are PRIMARILY responsible for correctness of the amount; if any amount is drawn in excess, the drawing officer must make good the excess; counter-signing officers and Treasury Officers are jointly liable for losses arising from culpable negligence.
verification_method: Audit trail: drawing/counter-signing/treasury officers' signatures recorded on bill; recovery action if over-payment detected

rule_text: AP administrative authorities should NOT ordinarily consider any representation/protest against a recovery ordered by the Accountant-General unless received within THREE MONTHS from the date the Government servant making the representation received first intimation of the order.
verification_method: Reject post-3-month representations against AG-ordered recoveries unless extraordinary justification on file
